title: C4OS Grill Question 005: Terminal Allowlist
description: This question pins down the config-backed read-only command allowlist for the agent-owned command terminal. Submit the form, then paste the generated response back into chat.

questions.label: Where should the default command allowlist live?
options: App-bundled default config, overrideable by workspace config
Hardcoded in app code for MVP
questions.help: Recommended neutral baseline: app-bundled defaults with workspace override, so MVP has safe defaults while project-specific tools can be added.

questions.label: How should C4OS decide a command is read-only?
options: Only exact allowlisted command patterns count as read-only
Infer read-only from command names and flags
Ask the runtime/model to classify read-only commands
All commands without shell redirection are read-only
questions.help: This must be deterministic enough to enforce before execution.

questions.label: Which commands belong in the MVP default allowlist?
questions.help: Select only commands that may run without approval when scoped to the active project.

questions.label: Clarifications or exclusions
questions.help: Add rules for redirection, pipes, package installs, network access, test commands, git commands, or command output limits.
